summary: Added the first HTTP auth flow with bcrypt password verification, JWT access tokens, an authenticated `/me` endpoint, and integration coverage that keeps soft-deleted accounts inaccessible.
reason: The auth module only had persistence primitives, so the next bounded-context step was to expose a secure login flow and a reusable auth guard without leaking business logic into Gin handlers or changing unrelated domains.
risk: Medium. Authentication behavior now depends on configured JWT secrets and password-hash correctness, and protected requests will reject accounts that become soft deleted after a token is issued.
notes: The current schema has no session table, so this batch intentionally uses stateless JWT access tokens only and documents logout/revocation as future work.
The `/api/v1/auth/me` guard re-reads the active account from PostgreSQL so a soft-deleted user cannot keep using an already-issued token.
Password hashing and verification live in `pkg/crypto` as reusable primitives, while auth orchestration stays inside the auth module boundaries.
